permitted_trigger_types.label: Generic answer not anchored to submission
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate gives a correct but generic answer that could apply to any case, not specifically to their submission.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Cannot explain a key decision from the submission
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate fails to explain why a specific decision in the submission was made.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Response contradicts the submission
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate's response materially conflicts with what is written in the submission.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Avoids reflection or glosses over uncertainty
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate gives a superficial or evasive answer when asked to reflect.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Unfamiliar with patient-specific details
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate appears not to know specific facts from their own case.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Vague or unsupported recommendation
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate states a conclusion without linking it to the case or providing rationale.

prohibited_followup_reasons: Seeking completeness for its own sake
followup_language_principles: Anchor every follow-up to the submission: 'In your case...' or 'Your submission states...'
Ask one focused question, not a list
Do not signal whether the candidate is right or wrong
Use neutral framing: 'Walk me through your reasoning on X in this case'
